article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: The model used non-approved bias type labels, produced several false positives, and flagged at least one attributed/quoted phrase as author bias. It caught one real framing instance in the author’s prose but missed other clear author-level spin/word-choice.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: It improperly flagged the attributed/quoted 'two-speed system' language, violating the attribution rule; other items were not direct quotes.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged passages were neutral reporting or structural/source mentions, not genuine author-prose bias. One item referenced reliance on ISA without clear bias in the cited text; another inferred 'corporate bias' from a factual product mention.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: It missed multiple author-level biases such as loaded phrasing ('interrogate', 'put them on the spot'), colloquial framing ('hammering in the lesson'), and positive qualifiers ('innovative ways'). It did catch the opening framing.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All labels used (e.g., 'Selection and Omission Bias', 'Source Bias', 'Framing Bias', 'Corporate Bias') are outside the allowed set; even where content was valid, types were incorrect.
